title: Fixed log prob result generation

step_plan.summary: Fixes `/v1/completions` non-streaming responses so that when a completion arrives in multiple incremental chunks, logprob arrays (`tokens`, `token_logprobs`, `top_logprobs`) are merged/appended per choice index instead of being overwritten, producing token-by-token logprobs consistent with the final concatenated text.

steps.operation: Fix non-streaming aggregation to merge per-chunk logprobs per choice index instead of overwriting
steps.reason: `request_completion` aggregates multiple engine chunks into one final `/v1/completions` response. It currently assigns `logprob_results[idx] = choice.logprobs` on each chunk, which drops earlier token logprobs for the same `choice.index`. Replace this with an append/merge strategy so `tokens`, `token_logprobs`, and `top_logprobs` accumulate in order across chunks (matching the accumulated `text`).
steps.side_effects: Changes the shape/length of `choices[i].logprobs.*` in non-streaming responses (now includes all generated tokens instead of last chunk only). Requires ensuring list lengths stay aligned and may expose any upstream chunk irregularities (e.g., empty token entries). Add/adjust any response validation or tests that assumed single-entry logprobs.

steps.operation: Normalize/validate per-delta logprobs structure to guarantee merge safety (list alignment, None handling, empty deltas)
steps.reason: Merging relies on consistent per-chunk `CompletionLogProbs` objects. Ensure `get_logprobs_from_delta` always returns either `None` or an object whose `tokens`, `token_logprobs`, and `top_logprobs` are all present and of equal length (or safely default to empty lists). This prevents merge-time mismatches when some deltas omit logprob fields or produce empty/placeholder tokens.
steps.side_effects: May alter how missing/partial logprob data is represented (e.g., returning empty lists vs. None), which can affect both streaming and non-streaming output. Could require updates to any callers that check for `None` or assume specific list contents.

steps.operation: Ensure final response wrapping preserves merged logprobs without re-shaping/overwriting and keeps invariants intact
steps.reason: After aggregation, `wrap_completion_response` constructs the final OpenAI completion payload. Verify it passes through the merged `logprob_results` as-is per `choice.index`, does not reinitialize/overwrite logprobs, and (optionally) enforces basic invariants (equal lengths across token arrays) before serializing.
steps.side_effects: If invariants are enforced here, invalid upstream logprob chunks could now fail fast (raising/returning an error) instead of silently producing malformed output. This may require updating tests or error handling in the entrypoint.
